context: Tests whether the agent implements the quick view modal using a native <dialog> element with showModal()/close(), an accessible loading skeleton, backdrop-click-to-close, and WCAG-compliant focus return on close.

checklist.name: Uses native <dialog> element
checklist.description: QuickViewModal.jsx renders a <dialog> HTML element as the modal container (not a <div> with role="dialog" or similar)

checklist.name: Uses showModal() to open
checklist.description: QuickViewModal.jsx calls dialogRef.current.showModal() (or equivalent ref-based call) to open the dialog, not setAttribute or className toggling

checklist.name: Uses dialog.close() to close
checklist.description: QuickViewModal.jsx calls dialogRef.current.close() (or equivalent) to programmatically close the dialog

checklist.name: Loading skeleton shown while fetching
checklist.description: QuickViewModal.jsx (or useQuickView.js) renders a skeleton/placeholder UI element when the product data is still loading (loading state is true)

checklist.name: aria-live on loading element
checklist.description: The loading skeleton element has aria-live="polite" attribute

checklist.name: aria-label on loading element
checklist.description: The loading skeleton element has an aria-label attribute describing the loading state (e.g. 'Loading product details')

checklist.name: Backdrop click closes modal
checklist.description: QuickViewModal.jsx handles click events on the dialog element itself and calls onClose when e.target is the dialog element (not its children)

checklist.name: Trigger element reference stored
checklist.description: The component or hook stores a ref to the trigger element (button that opened the modal) before or at the time of opening

checklist.name: Focus returned via requestAnimationFrame
checklist.description: On close, focus is returned to the trigger element using requestAnimationFrame (e.g. requestAnimationFrame(() => triggerRef.current?.focus()) or similar)

checklist.name: Fetch error closes modal
checklist.description: useQuickView.js (or equivalent) handles fetch errors by setting isOpen to false (closing the modal) rather than leaving it open in a broken state

checklist.name: Hook sets loading: true on open before fetch resolves
checklist.description: useQuickView.js sets loading to true immediately when openQuickView is called, before the fetch completes
